alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed framework can be used to classify books according to their style and publication dates. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.rationale: Both claims assert the framework discriminates book characteristics (genre/style and publication date); the publication frames this as discrimination of characteristics rather than an explicit 'classification' task.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed framework can characterize documents based on their semantic flow. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed machine listening techniques improve the generalizability of sound event detection models across heterogeneous acoustic environments.

alignments.rationale: The preprint claim is a broad statement about improved generalizability; the publication attributes this specifically to PCEN reducing temporal overfitting, which narrows and clarifies the mechanism.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The introduction of noise adaptation techniques, including per-channel energy normalization and a context-adaptive neural network layer, enhances the performance of the sound event detection system.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Context adaptation on PCEN-based summary statistics reduces spatial overfitting across sensor locations.
alignments.rationale: The preprint names multiple noise-adaptation techniques; the publication emphasizes the effect of context adaptation applied to PCEN summaries, making the claim more specific (spatial overfitting reduction).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The combination of the proposed techniques yields state-of-the-art results that surpass those achieved by artificial data augmentation alone.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Combining PCEN and context adaptation yields state-of-the-art results that are unmatched by artificial data augmentation alone.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the preprint claim with essentially the same scope and strength about combined techniques outperforming artificial augmentation.

alignments.pub_claim_text: EARNN utilizes a serialized long short-term memory to learn unified representations of Q&A, incorporating attention mechanisms to capture topic effects. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Same technical approach described; publication slightly rewords but preserves the claim and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed time-sensitive ranking function models the timeliness in community question answering. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A time-sensitive ranking function is designed within EARNN to model the timeliness aspect in community question answering. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same designed function and purpose with consistent certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Extensive experiments on a real-world dataset from Quora validate the effectiveness and interpretability of the EARNN model. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Extensive experiments on a real-world dataset from Quora validate the effectiveness and interpretability of the EARNN model. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The experimental validation claim appears verbatim and unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The coupled Variational Auto-Encoder (VAE) method improves the accuracy and robustness of probabilistic inferences on represented data. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The coupled variational autoencoder (VAE) method improves the accuracy and robustness of the model representation of handwritten numeral images. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Publication specifies the domain (handwritten numeral images), narrowing the general preprint claim while preserving the conclusion and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The output images generated by the coupled VAE method are clearer and less blurry compared to those generated by the traditional VAE algorithm. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The coupled VAE improves the reconstruction likelihood, especially when seeded with corrupted images. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes reconstruction likelihood and robustness on corrupted inputs rather than directly stating visual clarity; the core improvement claim is retained but reframed toward likelihood/robustness.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The latent variable in the coupled VAE model has a smaller deviation and a more compact latent space, which generates improved output values. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The divergence between the posterior and prior distribution of the latent distribution is reduced with the coupled VAE. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication gives a more precise statistical description (reduced posterior–prior divergence) and states it with higher certainty than the preprint's more qualitative phrasing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The neutral accuracy, measured by the geometric mean, is improved in the coupled VAE model. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: All five corruptions evaluated showed improvement in reconstruction accuracy and robustness. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports improvements across multiple corruptions in reconstruction accuracy/robustness, which corresponds to the preprint's stated gains in neutral (geometric-mean) accuracy.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The robust accuracy, measured by the -2/3 generalized mean, is also improved in the coupled VAE model. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: All five corruptions evaluated showed improvement in reconstruction accuracy and robustness. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication's general statement about improved reconstruction robustness across corruptions maps to the preprint's specific robust-accuracy metric claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Source constraints can have a dramatic impact on disclosure analysis in data integration systems. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Source constraints can have a dramatic impact on disclosure analysis. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Publication restates the same conclusion, slightly shortened but equivalent in meaning and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The paper studies the problem of determining whether a given data integration system discloses a source query to an attacker in the presence of constraints. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The paper studies the problem of determining whether a given data integration system discloses a source query to an attacker in the presence of constraints. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The formulation of the studied problem is unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The authors provide both lower and upper bounds on source-aware disclosure analysis. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The authors provide both lower and upper bounds on source-aware disclosure analysis. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The technical result about bounds is preserved verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method utilizes the characteristics of the hierarchical structure of the video to perform multi-level feature extraction.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method based on a deep convolutional graph neural network (DCGN) utilizes the hierarchical structure of the video for multi-level feature extraction.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same contribution with near-identical wording and emphasis on hierarchical multi-level feature extraction.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The novel video classification method based on a deep convolutional graph neural network (DCGN) outperforms RNN based benchmarks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results of the DCGN method outperform RNN based benchmarks on the YouTube-8M Large-Scale Video Understanding dataset.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the performance claim versus RNN benchmarks but specifies the dataset (YouTube-8M), narrowing the scope to that evaluation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Unsupervised solutions like feature average pooling have limited ability to represent the video.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not reiterate or discuss the limitation of unsupervised pooling approaches, so this conclusion was omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: RNN based models experience decreased performance due to the long video length and hierarchical relationships between frames.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results of the DCGN method outperform RNN based benchmarks on the YouTube-8M Large-Scale Video Understanding dataset.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the empirical comparison to RNNs but removes the causal explanation about long video length and hierarchy, so the original mechanistic claim is revised and less strongly supported.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A large-scale comprehensive semantic network of technology-related data, named TechNet, has been constructed to support engineering knowledge discovery and technology search.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A large-scale comprehensive semantic network of technology-related data, named TechNet, has been constructed to support engineering knowledge discovery and technology search.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same contribution claim verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: TechNet covers elemental concepts in all domains of technology and their semantic associations by mining the complete U.S. patent database from 1976.
alignments.pub_claim_text: TechNet covers elemental concepts in all domains of technology and their semantic associations by mining the complete U.S. patent database from 1976.
alignments.rationale: The result is restated unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Natural language processing techniques and recent word embedding algorithms were utilized to extract terms and establish their semantic relationships within TechNet.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Natural language processing techniques and recent word embedding algorithms were utilized to extract terms and establish their semantic relationships within TechNet.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the methodological claim exactly.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: TechNet may serve as an infrastructure for various applications, including technical text summaries and relational knowledge discovery, in engineering and technology.
alignments.pub_claim_text: TechNet may serve as an infrastructure for various applications, including technical text summaries and relational knowledge discovery, in engineering and technology.
alignments.rationale: The publication carries over the same potential-application conclusion without change in scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: TechNet is made public via an online interface and APIs for users to retrieve technology-related terms and their relevancies.
alignments.pub_claim_text: TechNet is made public via an online interface and APIs for users to retrieve technology-related terms and their relevancies.
alignments.rationale: The availability and access claim is unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The solution to the interpolation problem is a regular Radon-Nikodym derivative.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The solution to the interpolation problem is a regular Radon-Nikodym derivative.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same theoretical result verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: In the Radon-Nikodym approach, both outcome probabilities and the probability space change with new observations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: In the Radon-Nikodym approach, both outcome probabilities and the probability space change with new observations.
alignments.rationale: The conceptual conclusion is preserved exactly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Lebesgue quadrature technique can be applied to the optimal clustering problem.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Lebesgue quadrature technique can be applied to the optimal clustering problem.
alignments.rationale: The publication affirms the same applicability result without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A distinguishing feature of the Radon-Nikodym approach is the knowledge of the invariant group, which ensures that all answers are invariant to any non-degenerated linear transform of input vector components.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A distinguishing feature of the Radon-Nikodym approach is the knowledge of the invariant group, which ensures that all answers are invariant to any non-degenerated linear transform of input vector x components.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same invariant-group claim but clarifies wording by explicitly naming the input vector (x); the substantive meaning is the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A software product implementing the algorithms of interpolation, classification, and optimal clustering is available from the authors.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A software product implementing the algorithms of interpolation, classification, and optimal clustering is available from the authors.
alignments.rationale: The software-availability claim appears in the publication but with reduced reported certainty, so the claim is preserved but with tempered confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Hybrid crops that are tolerant to drought and heat stress produce more consistent yields compared to hybrids that are not tolerant to these stresses.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Hybrid crops that are tolerant to drought and heat stress produce more consistent yields compared to hybrids that are not tolerant to these stresses.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same agronomic conclusion verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study presents an unsupervised approach that successfully classified corn hybrids based on their tolerance to drought and heat stress.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The unsupervised approach presented in this paper was recognized as one of the winners in the 2019 Syngenta Crop Challenge.
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes external recognition (competition winner) rather than explicitly stating the classification-success result; the original classification claim is replaced by a related but different contribution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results labeled 121 hybrids as drought tolerant, 193 as heat tolerant, and 29 as tolerant to both stresses.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our results labeled 121 hybrids as drought tolerant, 193 as heat tolerant and 29 as tolerant to both stresses.
alignments.rationale: The numerical labeling results are reproduced exactly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed ordinal bucketing algorithm approximately generates $q$-quantiles from an incremental data stream.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed ordinal bucketing algorithm approximately generates q-quantiles from an incremental data stream.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the same contribution claim with equivalent wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The ordinal bucketing algorithm improves time and space complexity in Ordinal Monte Carlo Tree Search (OMCTS) when dealing with noisy rewards.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method yields better bounds on time and space complexity in Ordinal Monte Carlo Tree Search, especially in the presence of noisy rewards.
alignments.rationale: The publication affirms the same improvement in time/space complexity for OMCTS under noise, restated as better theoretical bounds.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results indicate that OMCTS with the proposed bucketing method outperforms vanilla Monte Carlo Tree Search in terms of time and space complexity under noise conditions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results demonstrate that the proposed method is dominant over vanilla Monte Carlo Tree Search in the presence of noise.
alignments.rationale: The publication strengthens the empirical claim to state dominance over vanilla MCTS under noise, increasing confidence and clarity of the comparative result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: GDNN (neural) PID controllers perform well on a range of control systems.
alignments.pub_claim_text: GDNN (neural) PID controllers perform well on a range of complex control systems.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same result but adds the adjective "complex," which clarifies/specifies the domain of systems (thus narrowing scope) without changing confidence in the result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The neural PID controller performs better than standard PID control in 15 of 16 tasks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The neural PID controller performs better than standard PID control in 15 of 16 tasks.
alignments.rationale: The claim is reproduced verbatim in the publication with no change in wording, scope, or expressed confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The neural PID controller performs better than model-based control in 13 of 16 tasks.
alignments.rationale: No corresponding claim about outperforming model-based control appears in the publication; the claim present in the preprint is omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Bounded-input bounded-output stability analysis is used to evaluate the parameters suggested by the neural network, enhancing interpretability.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The use of bounded-input bounded-output stability analysis makes the parameters suggested by the neural network understandable for engineers.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the same contribution to emphasize understandability for engineers, clarifying the practical interpretability claim without changing its substance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The combination of rigorous evaluation and better interpretability is an important step towards the acceptance of neural-network-based control approaches.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This combination of rigorous evaluation paired with better interpretability is an important step towards the acceptance of neural-network-based control approaches for real-world systems.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the conclusion but adds "for real-world systems," which specifies the target application (narrowing the scope) while keeping the overall conclusion and confidence intact.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This paper reviews different vehicle re-identification (V-reID) methods, categorizing them into sensor based, hybrid, and vision based methods.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This paper reviews different vehicle re-identification (V-reID) methods, including sensor based, hybrid, and vision based methods.
alignments.rationale: The publication reproduces the review categorization claim with equivalent meaning and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The review systematically addresses and evaluates vision based V-reID methods for the first time.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The review systematically addresses and evaluates vision based V-reID methods for the first time.
alignments.rationale: Identical claim retained in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experiments conducted on four comprehensive benchmark datasets compare the performances of recent hand-crafted feature based methods and deep feature based methods.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experiments conducted on four comprehensive benchmark datasets compare the performances of recent hand-crafted feature based methods and deep feature based methods.
alignments.rationale: The experimental setup and comparison claim is unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The analyses provide objective insight into the strengths and weaknesses of hand-crafted feature based methods and deep feature based methods.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The analyses provide objective insight into the strengths and weaknesses of hand-crafted feature based methods and deep feature based methods.
alignments.rationale: The analytical conclusion appears verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The paper critically discusses the challenges and future trends of V-reID methods.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The paper critically discusses the challenges and future trends of V-reID methods.
alignments.rationale: The discussion of challenges and future trends is preserved without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A model-based metric for estimating the factual accuracy of generated text is proposed.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A model-based metric for estimating the factual accuracy of generated text is proposed.
alignments.rationale: The core contribution is identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A new large-scale dataset based on Wikipedia and Wikidata has been introduced and released for training relation classifiers and end-to-end fact extraction models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A new large-scale dataset based on Wikipedia and Wikidata has been introduced and released for training relation classifiers and end-to-end fact extraction models.
alignments.rationale: The dataset release claim is reproduced exactly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: End-to-end models can extract complete sets of facts from datasets containing full pages of text.
alignments.pub_claim_text: End-to-end models can extract complete sets of facts from datasets containing full pages of text.
alignments.rationale: The result about end-to-end fact extraction is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The efficacy of multiple models estimating factual accuracy on a Wikipedia text summarization task is shown to be superior compared to ROUGE and other model-free variants.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The efficacy of multiple models estimating factual accuracy on a Wikipedia text summarization task is shown to be superior compared to ROUGE and other model-free variants.
alignments.rationale: The comparative efficacy claim is retained with the same framing in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed two-stage multiscale generative adversarial neural network enables a decrease in electron beam coverage by 17.9 times with a 3.8% test set root mean squared intensity error for spiral scans.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed two-stage multiscale generative adversarial neural network enables electron beam coverage to be decreased by 17.9× with a 3.8% test set root mean squared intensity error.
alignments.rationale: The quantitative result is preserved in the publication with equivalent meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The network achieves a decrease in electron beam coverage by 87.0 times with a 6.2% error for mean squared error based pre-training.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The two-stage multiscale generative adversarial neural network allows for a decrease in electron beam coverage by 87.0× with a 6.2% error for mean squared error based pre-training.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same quantitative pre-training result without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The generator networks are trained on partial scans created from a new dataset of 16227 scanning transmission electron micrographs.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The generator networks are trained on partial scans created from a new dataset of 16227 scanning transmission electron micrographs.
alignments.rationale: The dataset and training setup claim is identical in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: High performance is achieved with adaptive learning rate clipping of loss spikes and an auxiliary trainer network.
alignments.pub_claim_text: High performance is achieved with adaptive learning rate clipping of loss spikes and an auxiliary trainer network.
alignments.rationale: The methodological conclusion is preserved as stated.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Neural language modeling approaches can effectively embed tabular data into vector spaces.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Neural language modeling approaches can effectively embed tabular data into vector spaces.
alignments.rationale: The contribution about embedding tabular data is unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Different table elements, such as captions, column headings, and cells, are considered for training word and entity embeddings.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Different table elements, such as captions, column headings, and cells, are considered for training word and entity embeddings.
alignments.rationale: The treatment of table elements for embeddings is preserved verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Table embeddings significantly improve the performance of state-of-the-art baselines in table-related tasks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Table embeddings significantly improve the performance of state-of-the-art baselines in table-related tasks.
alignments.rationale: The reported improvement via table embeddings is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed scaffold-based molecular generative model guarantees that the generated molecules retain the given scaffold with certainty. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our proposed graph generative model guarantees that the generated molecules contain the scaffold with certainty. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Wording is effectively identical: both assert a guarantee that generated molecules contain the scaffold with high certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The evaluation of the model using unseen scaffolds showed the validity, uniqueness, and novelty of generated molecules as high as the case using seen scaffolds. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The learned rule of extending molecules can generalize to arbitrary kinds of scaffolds, including those unseen during learning. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the empirical result as a broader generalization claim about learned rules applying to arbitrary/unseen scaffolds, expanding the original experimental finding.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model can generalize the learned chemical rules of adding atoms and bonds rather than simply memorizing the mapping from scaffolds to molecules during learning. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The learned rule of extending molecules can generalize to arbitrary kinds of scaffolds, including those unseen during learning. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Both statements assert the model learns generalizable rules rather than memorizing scaffold–molecule mappings; the publication expresses this as generalization to arbitrary/unseen scaffolds.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Despite the restraint of fixing core structures, the model could simultaneously control multiple molecular properties when generating new molecules. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our model can simultaneously control multiple chemical properties in the conditional generation of molecules. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claims are congruent: both state the model can control multiple properties during conditional generation despite fixed cores.

alignments.pub_claim_text: We demonstrate that our model can employ a simple semi-supervised extension to broaden its applicability to situations with limited data. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This publication claim (semi-supervised extension) is not present in the preprint and therefore is an added contribution in the paper.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SELFIES is a string-based representation of molecules that is 100% robust, meaning every SELFIES string corresponds to a valid molecule. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: SELFIES is a string-based representation of molecules that is 100% robust, ensuring every string corresponds to a valid molecule. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the exact property of SELFIES robustness; meaning is identical.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SELFIES can represent every molecule and can be directly applied in arbitrary machine learning models without the adaptation of the models. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: SELFIES can represent every molecule and can be directly applied in arbitrary machine learning models without the adaptation of the models. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Identical claim about representational completeness and model applicability.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: In experiments, the model using SELFIES stores two orders of magnitude more diverse molecules than a similar test with SMILES. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: In experiments, the internal memory of models using SELFIES stores two orders of magnitude more diverse molecules than similar tests with SMILES. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Publication clarifies that the two-orders-of-magnitude gain refers specifically to internal memory storage, but the reported experimental result is the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The use of SELFIES allows for explanation and interpretation of the internal workings of the generative models since all generated molecules are valid. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The validity of all molecules represented by SELFIES allows for explanation and interpretation of the internal workings of the generative models. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Both statements tie SELFIES' guarantee of validity to improved explainability/interpretability of generative models.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed DDPNAS framework achieves state-of-the-art performance on various computer vision tasks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed DDPNAS framework achieves state-of-the-art performance on various computer vision tasks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same high-level performance claim without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DDPNAS facilitates a theoretical bound on accuracy and efficiency. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DDPNAS facilitates a theoretical bound on accuracy and efficiency. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution claim is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The architectures searched by DDPNAS achieve top-1 accuracies of 97.56 on CIFAR-10 and 77.2 on ImageNet (mobile settings). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The architectures searched by DDPNAS achieve top-1 accuracies of 97.56 on CIFAR-10 and 77.2 on ImageNet (mobile settings). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Identical numerical performance claims appear in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The search process of DDPNAS is completed in only 1.8 GPU hours on a Tesla V100. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The search process of DDPNAS is completed in only 1.8 GPU hours on a Tesla V100. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The reported runtime/efficiency claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The kernel mean embedding of probability distributions allows for the definition of a distance measure between distributions called maximum mean discrepancy (MMD). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not state the MMD-definition claim present in the preprint; instead it focuses on quantum representations and uniqueness results, so this preprint claim is omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Representing probability distributions in a pure quantum state enables an explicit representation of the mean embedding. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed quantum representation allows for an explicit representation of the mean embedding, unlike classical methods that rely on an implicit representation. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Both state the core contribution: a quantum pure-state representation gives an explicit mean embedding, contrasting with classical implicit representations.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The explicit representation of mean embeddings can speed up methods that rely on inner products. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The explicit representation of mean embeddings can speed up methods that rely on inner products. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The speedup claim is carried over directly from preprint to publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There are theoretical and experimental challenges that need to be solved to achieve speedups in methods using mean embeddings. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The work discusses the theoretical and experimental challenges that need to be solved to achieve speedups in methods using mean embeddings. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: Both versions discuss the same set of challenges/limitations for achieving speedups.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The representation of probability distributions in a pure quantum state is unique if the corresponding kernel function is c0 universal. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This uniqueness result (c0-universal kernel condition) appears in the publication but was not present in the preprint, so it is an added theoretical claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed theory guarantees that hierarchical networks are provably scale covariant. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed theory guarantees that hierarchical networks are provably scale covariant. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Identical theoretical guarantee in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The general sufficiency argument for obtaining scale covariance applies to a wide class of networks defined from linear and non-linear differential expressions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The general sufficiency argument for obtaining scale covariance applies to a wide class of networks defined from linear and nonlinear differential expressions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The sufficiency argument claim is preserved with only minor wording normalization (non-linear -> nonlinear).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The resulting representation from the constructed network allows for scale and rotation covariance. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The resulting representation from the constructed network allows for scale and rotation covariance. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Same claim about combined scale and rotation covariance is present in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A simplified mean-reduced representation of the QuasiQuadNet leads to promising experimental results on three texture datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A simplified mean-reduced representation of the QuasiQuadNet leads to promising experimental results on three texture datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Experimental result and dataset count are identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed dlADMM algorithm reduces the time complexity from cubic to quadratic in (latent) feature dimensions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed dlADMM algorithm reduces the time complexity from cubic to quadratic in (latent) feature dimensions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication claim is verbatim the same as the preprint claim, expressing the same result and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The dlADMM algorithm provides the first proof of global convergence for an ADMM-based method in a deep neural network problem under mild conditions. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The dlADMM method provides the first proof of global convergence for an ADMM-based method in a deep neural network problem under mild conditions. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same contribution claim with equivalent wording and the same level of certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experiments on benchmark datasets demonstrated that the dlADMM algorithm outperforms most of the comparison methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experiments on benchmark datasets demonstrated that the dlADMM algorithm outperforms most of the comparison methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The experimental result claim is carried over identically from preprint to publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ADMM faces challenges such as lack of global convergence guarantees, slow convergence towards solutions, and cubic time complexity with regard to feature dimensions. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: ADMM faces challenges such as lack of global convergence guarantees, slow convergence, and cubic time complexity with regard to feature dimensions. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same limitations with only minor wording differences ("slow convergence towards solutions" vs "slow convergence").

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We achieve near perfect performance on classifying unseen images from SOT (approximately 99.9%) in 4.66 seconds. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We achieve near perfect performance on classifying unseen images from SOT (≈ 99.9%) in 4.66 seconds. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The published claim matches the preprint claim exactly in content and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed scheme has a superior performance with lower complexity compared to local search-based greedy sparse phase retrieval.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed scheme has a superior performance with lower complexity compared to local search-based greedy sparse phase retrieval.
alignments.rationale: The claim is reproduced verbatim in the publication with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The DNN allows for the estimation of the extended support set $\extmath{E}$ with a short running time.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The DNN allows for the estimation of the extended support set with a short running time.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same result about short running time for extended support estimation without changing scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Support $\extmath{T}$ can be determined from the DNN output rather than from the full index set.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Support can be determined from the DNN output rather than from the full index set by applying hard thresholding to the extended support set.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the original claim but adds an explicit mechanism (hard thresholding) for determining support, which clarifies the method without changing scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The paper presents a publish-subscribe-based approach to develop test applications for failure diagnosis in self-organizing multiagent systems.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed approach based on metadata and the publish-subscribe paradigm effectively addresses the process of failure diagnosis in self-organizing multiagent systems.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same core contribution but adds that it uses metadata and frames it as effectively addressing failure diagnosis, which clarifies and slightly strengthens the description.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed tests can detect failures at both the global behavior of the system and the local properties of its parts.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach allows for testing self-organizing multiagent systems in the context of both local and global behavior.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same capability to test both local and global behaviors with equivalent meaning and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A self-organizing multiagent system was developed to simulate smart street lights that interact to reduce energy consumption and maintain visual comfort.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed self-organizing multiagent system simulates smart street lights that interact to achieve global goals of reducing energy consumption and maintaining visual comfort.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the description of the developed system and its objectives with no substantive change in meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The street lights achieve global behaviors through local behaviors that are developed automatically via a self-organizing process based on machine learning algorithms.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The street lights in the system automatically develop local behaviors through a self-organizing process based on machine-learning algorithms.
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes automatic development of local behaviors but omits the explicit claim that these local behaviors achieve global behaviors; the wording is more focused and the claim's certainty is raised.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeLTA is the first analytical model that accurately estimates the traffic at each GPU memory hierarchy level.
alignments.pub_claim_text: DeLTA is the first analytical model that accurately estimates the traffic at each GPU memory hierarchy level.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same contribution claim verbatim, preserving scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model accounts for the complex reuse patterns of a parallel convolution algorithm.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model accounts for the complex reuse patterns of a parallel convolution algorithm.
alignments.rationale: Identical claim in publication; no change in meaning or scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our model is both accurate and robust for different CNNs and GPU architectures.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model is both accurate and robust for different CNNs and GPU architectures.
alignments.rationale: Result claim is preserved exactly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model can be used to carefully balance the scaling of different GPU resources for efficient CNN performance improvement.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model can be used to carefully balance the scaling of different GPU resources for efficient CNN performance improvement.
alignments.rationale: Conclusion preserved unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study discovers interpretable local Hebbian learning rules that enable autonomous global learning in artificial neural networks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study discovers interpretable local Hebbian learning rules that enable autonomous global learning in artificial neural networks.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the contribution identically, preserving interpretation and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The evolved local Hebbian learning rules converge into a set of well-defined interpretable types.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The evolved local Hebbian learning rules converge into a set of well-defined interpretable types.
alignments.rationale: Claim is reproduced without change in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The performance of the evolved rules during online lifetime learning tasks is comparable to that of offline learning methods such as hill climbing.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The performance of the evolved rules during online lifetime learning tasks is comparable to that of offline learning methods such as hill climbing.
alignments.rationale: Performance comparison claim is unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep Multiset Canonical Correlation Analysis (dMCCA) effectively recovers the common signal across different modalities corrupted by multiplicative and additive noise.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A neural method based on multiview correlation can effectively capture the information shared across a large number of views by subsampling them in a view-agnostic manner during training.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the claim more generally in terms of multiview correlation and view-agnostic subsampling rather than the original emphasis on multiplicative/additive noise; this broadens and shifts emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: dMCCA can be trained for complex data using mini-batches without the need for class information.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results and analysis underscore the applicability of the proposed method for a view-agnostic learning setting.
alignments.rationale: The publication frames training without class information as applicability to a view-agnostic setting; this clarifies the original training claim though with less technical detail.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Performance evaluation shows that dMCCA outperforms other CCA-based approaches and is comparable to deep neural network models trained end-to-end on noisy handwritten datasets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experiments demonstrate the robustness of view bootstrapping to model a large number of views across various audio and visual tasks.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports experimental robustness across different task domains and many views rather than the specific noisy handwritten comparisons in the preprint, shifting and broadening the experimental claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model's sensitivity to recover correlated components is analyzed with respect to mini-batch size and dimension of the embeddings.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The error of the bootstrapped multiview correlation objective can be analyzed using matrix concentration theory to provide an upper bound on the number of views to subsample for a given embedding dimension.
alignments.rationale: The publication formalizes the sensitivity analysis into a theoretical error analysis and explicit bound related to embedding dimension and subsampling, which clarifies and makes the preprint analysis more precise.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed preference neural network (PNN) addresses the problem of indifference preferences orders with a new activation function.
alignments.pub_claim_text: PNN uses spearman correlation gradient ascent and two new activation functions to accelerate ranking.
alignments.rationale: The publication affirms the new activation-function contribution but changes details (mentions two functions and spearman gradient ascent), so the claim is revised rather than unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PNN solves the Multi-label ranking problem where labels may have indifference preference orders or subgroups are equally ranked.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Preference Neural Network (PNN) outperforms five previously proposed methods for label ranking.
alignments.rationale: The publication focuses on general label-ranking performance and omits the explicit emphasis on indifference/subgroup ranking present in the preprint, so the claim is narrowed/revised.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PNN outperforms five previously proposed methods for strict label ranking in terms of accurate results with high computational efficiency.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Preference Neural Network (PNN) outperforms five previously proposed methods for label ranking.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the core performance claim (outperforming five methods); some efficiency detail is reported elsewhere in the paper, so the main result is clarified rather than removed.

alignments.pub_claim_text: PN achieves promising results on CFAR-100 with high computational efficiency.
alignments.rationale: This dataset-specific efficiency/result claim does not appear in the preprint and is newly present in the publication.

alignments.pub_claim_text: PNN is proposed in two forms, fully connected simple Three layers and Preference Net (PN).
alignments.rationale: The publication introduces specific model forms that were not described in the preprint, so this is an added implementation detail.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A set of necessary constraints in the form of a system of non-linear partial differential equations must be satisfied for functions to be implemented on a given tree.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A set of necessary constraints in the form of a system of nonlinear partial differential equations must be satisfied for functions to be implemented on a given tree.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same necessary-constraints result with equivalent wording; no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The conditions described are sufficient for both analytic and bit-valued functions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The conditions described are sufficient in contexts of analytic and bit-valued functions.
alignments.rationale: The sufficiency claim is preserved verbatim between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There are relatively few discrete functions that can be explicitly enumerated in the context of bit-valued functions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: There are relatively few discrete functions that can be explicitly enumerated in the context of bit-valued functions.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the preprint's observation about the scarcity of enumerable discrete functions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The work connects the structure of computation graphs with the functions they can implement.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The work connects the structure of computation graphs with the functions they can implement.
alignments.rationale: This conceptual contribution is maintained without substantive change in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach allows for comparison of different neural network architectures in regard to their function spaces.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The work connects the structure of computation graphs with the functions they can implement.
alignments.rationale: The publication's connection between graph structure and implementable functions corresponds to the preprint's claim about comparing architectures' function spaces.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings have potential applications to neuroscience and computer science.
alignments.rationale: This applications-oriented statement appears in the publication but was not present in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed unified deep network can be effectively trained for hyperspectral images classification using only minimally labeled training data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed unified deep network can be effectively trained for hyperspectral images classification using only minimally labeled training data.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the main training-with-few-labels result without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The network is flexible and scalable enough to function across various transfer situations, including cross-dataset and intra-image.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The network is flexible and scalable enough to function across various transfer situations, including cross data set and intraimage.
alignments.rationale: The transferability and scalability claim is preserved in the publication with equivalent scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The learned deep joint spectral-spatial feature representation is more generic and robust than many joint spectral-spatial feature representations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The learned deep joint spectral–spatial feature representation is more generic and robust than many joint spectral–spatial feature representations.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the claim about the learned representation's generality and robustness.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Extensive comparative evaluations demonstrate that the proposed method significantly outperforms many state-of-the-art approaches on three popular datasets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method significantly outperforms many state-of-the-art approaches on three popular data sets.
alignments.rationale: The empirical outperforming result is preserved identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The novel layer introduced can be used as the output of pre-trained neural networks for classification.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The novel layer introduced can be used as the output of pre-trained neural networks for classification.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the contribution exactly.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This layer supports incremental learning and can be partially trained in real time on embedded devices.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This layer supports incremental learning and can be partially trained in real time on embedded devices.
alignments.rationale: The incremental-learning and real-time training claim is preserved without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experiments demonstrate that it is possible to design more flexible and faster-to-train Neural Networks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experiments demonstrate that it is possible to design more flexible and faster-to-train Neural Networks.
alignments.rationale: The experimental finding is unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The design of these Neural Networks comes at the cost of a slight decrease in accuracy.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The design of these Neural Networks comes at the cost of a slight decrease in accuracy.
alignments.rationale: The limitation (slight accuracy decrease) is present in both preprint and publication; framing changed to 'limitation' but the substantive claim is the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our differentially private approach for training neural networks effectively improves privacy loss accounting.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed differentially private approach for training neural networks effectively improves privacy loss accounting.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the privacy-accounting improvement claim from the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The implementation of a dynamic privacy budget allocator over the course of training improves model accuracy.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A dynamic privacy budget allocator is implemented to improve model accuracy during training.
alignments.rationale: The publication affirms the implemented dynamic allocator and its accuracy-improvement role as stated in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Extensive experiments demonstrate that our approach enhances training efficiency and model quality under a given privacy budget.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach enhances training efficiency and model quality under a given privacy budget.
alignments.rationale: The publication states the enhancement result more directly (dropping the explicit 'extensive experiments' phrasing), so the substantive result is clarified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The use of concentrated differential privacy (CDP) allows for a formal and refined privacy loss analysis.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The use of concentrated differential privacy (CDP) allows for a formal and refined privacy loss analysis.
alignments.rationale: The CDP-based formal analysis claim is preserved in the publication with equivalent content.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: 3D-BEVIS is a deep learning framework designed for 3D semantic instance segmentation on point clouds. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: 3D-BEVIS is a deep learning framework designed for joint semantic- and instance-segmentation on 3D point clouds. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same contribution with equivalent meaning ('semantic instance segmentation' vs 'joint semantic- and instance-segmentation'), so the claim is effectively unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model learns a feature embedding and groups the obtained feature space into semantic instances. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model learns a feature embedding and groups the obtained feature space into semantic instances. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this result verbatim, so there is no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Current point-based methods scale linearly with the number of points by processing local sub-parts of a scene individually. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Current point-based methods process local sub-parts of a full scene independently, which is less effective for instance segmentation. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method combines local point geometry with global context information from an intermediate bird's-eye view representation. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method combines local point geometry with global context information using an intermediate bird’s-eye view representation. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed interpretable network trained on the jet spectrum $S_{2}(R)$ achieves performance comparable to that of a convolutional neural network (CNN) trained on jet images. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed interpretable network trained on the jet spectrum S2(R) achieves performance comparable to that of a convolutional neural network (CNN) trained on jet images. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the performance comparison claim verbatim, so no change is present.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The interpretable network simplifies the number of inputs and complexity of architecture compared to the CNN classifier. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The interpretable network simplifies the number of inputs and complexity of the architecture compared to the CNN classifier. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: This contribution is carried forward identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The intermediate feature of the network is an infrared and collinear safe C-correlator that estimates the importance of a $S_{2}(R)$ deposit at an angular scale R in classification. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The intermediate feature of the network is an infrared and collinear safe C-correlator that estimates the importance of an S2(R) deposit at an angular scale R. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the technical characterization of the intermediate feature with only minor wording changes; the meaning is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The jet spectrum $S_{2}(R)$ can be derived from a functional Taylor series of an arbitrary jet classifier function of energy flows. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The spectrum S2(R) can be derived from a functional Taylor series of an arbitrary jet classifier function of energy flows. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this theoretical derivation claim with equivalent content.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The CDFNet demonstrates increased robustness compared to traditional deep learning networks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: FatSegNet demonstrates increased accuracy and robustness compared to traditional deep learning networks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication augments the original robustness claim by adding increased accuracy as well, changing the claim content and broadening its scope beyond robustness alone.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: FatSegNet dice score outperforms manual raters on the abdominal visceral adipose tissue (VAT, 0.828 vs. 0.788). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: FatSegNet Dice score outperforms manual raters on VAT and produces comparable results on SAT. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the VAT outperforming result verbatim while aggregating it with the SAT result; the VAT claim itself is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: FatSegNet produces comparable results on subcutaneous adipose tissue (SAT, 0.973 vs. 0.982). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: FatSegNet Dice score outperforms manual raters on VAT and produces comparable results on SAT. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication combines the SAT and VAT scoring results into one statement but preserves the comparable SAT outcome reported in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The pipeline has very small test-retest absolute percentage difference and excellent agreement between scan sessions (VAT: APD = 2.957%, ICC=0.998 and SAT: APD= 3.254%, ICC=0.996). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The pipeline has excellent agreement for both test-retest and manual re-editing. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the agreement claim for test-retest but generalizes it to include manual re-editing and omits the detailed numeric metrics, clarifying and broadening the scope of agreement described.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: FatSegNet can reliably analyze a 3D Dixon MRI in 1 min. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: FatSegNet can reliably analyze a 3D Dixon MRI in approximately 1 minute. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the practical runtime claim with only minor wording ('approximately'), so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Ising Born Machine (IBM) cannot, in the worst case, and up to suitable notions of error, be simulated efficiently by a classical device. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The circuits encountered during gradient-based training cannot be efficiently sampled from classically up to multiplicative error in the worst case. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies the hardness statement to the circuits arising during their gradient-based training and the sampling notion (multiplicative error), narrowing and clarifying the original broader claim about the IBM.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The cost functions suggested for training the IBM outperform the more commonly used Maximum Mean Discrepancy (MMD) for differentiable training. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The gradient-based training methods using Sinkhorn divergence and Stein discrepancy outperform the previous standard method that used maximum mean discrepancy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication names the specific divergences and retains the core result that these training cost functions outperform MMD, so the claim is unchanged in substance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed novel utilisation of quantum kernels provides improvements over its classical counterpart. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Quantum kernels are introduced to generative modelling in this work. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication makes a more conservative contribution claim (introducing quantum kernels to generative modelling) and does not assert explicit superiority over classical counterparts, so the original performance-improvement claim is softened.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The methods discussed have the potential to learn 'hard' quantum distributions, demonstrating the advantage of quantum over classical computers. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model demonstrates the ability to learn hard distributions and introduces formal definitions for 'quantum learning supremacy'. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the claim more strongly (demonstrates ability rather than just potential) and ties it to formal definitions, effectively amplifying and clarifying the original conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The paper provides the first formal definitions for what is called 'Quantum Learning Supremacy'. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model demonstrates the ability to learn hard distributions and introduces formal definitions for 'quantum learning supremacy'. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication explicitly includes the introduction of formal definitions as part of the consolidated claim, matching the preprint's contribution statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed nonlinear RGB-to-grayscale mapping improves robustness to appearance changes in vision-based localization systems. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A nonlinear RGB-to-grayscale mapping is learned to maximize the number of inlier feature matches for images captured under different lighting and weather conditions. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies the optimization objective (maximize inlier matches under varying conditions) while retaining the same practical effect (improved robustness), so the claim is clarified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Incorporating a learned low-dimensional context feature enhances cross-appearance feature matching. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Incorporating a learned low-dimensional context feature improves cross-appearance feature matching. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains this result with equivalent wording and emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Substantial improvements in localization performance are achieved across day-night cycles using the proposed method. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Substantial improvements in localization performance across day-night cycles are demonstrated, enabling continuous metric localization over a 30-hour period using a single mapping experience. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the substantial cross-appearance localization improvements and expands on their operational consequence, but the core performance claim remains the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach enables continuous metric localization over a 30-hour period using a single mapping experience. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Substantial improvements in localization performance across day-night cycles are demonstrated, enabling continuous metric localization over a 30-hour period using a single mapping experience. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication explicitly states the same continuous localization capability and embeds it within the demonstrated improvements, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed algorithm guarantees safety by leveraging Lipschitz-continuity to ensure that no unsafe states are visited during exploration. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed algorithm guarantees safety by leveraging Lipschitz-continuity to ensure that no unsafe states are visited during exploration. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The preprint and publication text are identical in wording and meaning, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The safety guarantee provided by the algorithm is deterministic, unlike many existing techniques. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The safety guarantee provided by the algorithm is deterministic, unlike many existing techniques. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The claim about determinism is verbatim in the publication, indicating no change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The algorithm is optimized to reduce the number of actions needed for exploring the safe space. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The algorithm is optimized to reduce the number of actions needed for exploring the safe space. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Contribution statement is identical between preprint and publication, so it is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The performance of the algorithm is demonstrated in comparison with baseline methods in simulation on navigation tasks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The performance of the algorithm is demonstrated in comparison with baseline methods in simulation on navigation tasks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The evaluation claim is reported verbatim in the publication, indicating no change.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Synthetic aperture sonar (SAS) imagery can generate high resolution images of the seafloor. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The Possibilistic Fuzzy Local Information C-Means (PFLICM) algorithm has been previously applied to segment SAS imagery. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The Res2Net block demonstrates consistent performance gains over baseline models on widely-used datasets like CIFAR-100 and ImageNet. (certainty=high, type=result)
